title: Flashcard Deck
description: A set of flashcards for the Flashcard Tester app. Three card types: (1) flashcard-only — question + string answer, no choices; (2) multiple-choice — question + integer answer (0-based index into choices) + choices array; (3) multi-select — question + integer array answer (all correct indices) + choices array.

meta.description: Optional metadata about the deck. Displayed in the app header when present.

description.description: Short summary of what the deck covers.

difficulty.description: Difficulty level — shown as a badge in the app.

license.description: The licence under which this card deck is published. Recommended: 'CC BY 4.0' for openly shared decks. Use a standard SPDX identifier or Creative Commons short-form (e.g. 'CC BY 4.0', 'CC BY-SA 4.0', 'CC0-1.0').

passMark.description: Minimum percentage score (1–100) required to pass. When set, the end-of-session summary shows a Passed or Failed verdict.

question.description: The question shown to the user.

answer.description: The correct answer. Three forms: (1) integer — 0-based index of the single correct choice (0 = first, 1 = second, etc.); (2) array of integers — all correct indices for multi-select questions, user must select exactly this set to be correct; (3) string — answer text for flashcard-only cards that have no choices.

oneOf.description: 0-based index of the single correct choice.
All correct indices for a multi-select question. User must select exactly this set.
Answer text for flashcard-only cards (no choices).

explanation.description: Shown after the user answers. Explains why the answer is correct and adds useful context.

link.description: Optional URL to source documentation or further reading.

choices.description: Answer options for multiple-choice or multi-select mode. Omit for plain flashcard mode. The answer field holds the 0-based index (or indices) of the correct choice(s).

meta.title: Example Deck
meta.description: A sample deck showing all three card types.

cards.question: What is the capital of France?
cards.explanation: Paris has been the capital of France since 987 AD and is home to the national government, the Élysée Palace, and the French parliament.

cards.question: Which of the following are primary colours? (Select all that apply)
cards.explanation: The primary colours in the traditional RYB colour model are red, yellow, and blue.

cards.question: Who wrote Hamlet?
cards.explanation: Hamlet was written by William Shakespeare around 1600–1601 and is one of his most famous tragedies, exploring themes of revenge, mortality, and betrayal.
